house_style.note_zh: 除非非常必要，少用固定机位；就算固定也尽量不要纯平视，要俯/仰/荷兰角等带角度机位。

decision_order: lookup strategy_matrix for preferred_move_families (motion first; static last resort)
map families to catalog keywords; pick prompt_en that fits subject
pick NON-eye-level angle from angles_lenses angle_by_emotion (+ subject bias)
apply composition_framing emotion_bias (visible frame facts for prompts)
write short movement.motivation (story reason, not pipeline jargon)
write look from three_point_and_motivated_light + lighting_for_emotion + look_bible
hard_rules: movement.motivation required and must be narrative (not 'because catalog said so')
prefer moving camera (push/pan/lateral/drift/creep/rack) over static_hold unless necessary
static_hold only when necessary (macro insert readability, explicit locked plate, brief force)
even if static: use slight_high / slight_low / low_angle / high_angle / dutch_* — avoid pure eye_level
do not default camera.angle to eye_level
dutch_angle / dutch_mild allowed when emotion is suspicion/oppression/dread or beat is disorientation — still write motivation
unmotivated orbit 360 / crash zoom / whip pan still avoided
do not rewrite dialogue or story
prefer movement.prompt_en from catalog when available
INSERT/object shots: do not invent human facial micro-acting
never use a catalog example sentence that contradicts subject (e.g. sleeping figure on envelope insert)
prefer motivated practical light language over empty 'cinematic lighting'
teaching_sources_internal: Motivated camera movement (film school language of movement)
M. David Mullen ASC — when/why to move the camera (dramatic / action / dimensional)
StudioBinder — camera movement types and storytelling use
StudioBinder / Adobe — shot size, angle, composition primers
Project house style: motion + angled camera (not flat TV eye-level locks)
